channel.title: Metals Industry News Feed
channel.description: Automated news feed for aluminum, steel, copper, and nickel industries

item.title: Copper Industry Report 2026: $184.5+ Bn Trends, Opportunities, Competitive Analysis and Long-term Forecasts
item.description: The copper market is projected to grow to $184.58 billion by 2030 with a CAGR of 10.4%, driven by electric vehicle production, renewable energy installations, and smart grid infrastructure. Key innovations include recycled copper products and advanced refining processes, with companies like Wieland-Werke AG introducing 'Cuprolife,' a 100% pure recycled copper tube for sustainable applications.

item.title: March 2026 Scrap Metal Prices: Copper & Aluminum Up, Steel Stable
item.description: Numerous aluminum scrap prices moved upward in early March, including alloy grades like 1100 Scrap, 3003 Scrap, and 6063 Extrusions. Prices for 5052 Scrap, 6061 Extrusions, E.C Aluminum Wire, Old Cast, UBC, Old Sheet, and Zorba 90% NF also increased. Mixed Aluminum Turnings and Aluminum Radiators edged up slightly, while some aluminum-based radiator categories remained unchanged.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: Aluminum prices are rising rapidly due to drying imports, tariffs, and global events, with no relief in sight. The update covers supply chain challenges, capacity constraints, and advice for contractors to use escalation clauses ahead of construction season. Midwest premiums have stabilized slightly but overall pricing pressure persists.

item.title: Supply fears lift LME aluminium to nearly four-year high
item.description: LME aluminum prices hit a nearly four-year high of $3,544 per metric ton due to Middle East conflict disrupting supplies through the Strait of Hormuz, which accounts for 9% of global production. Qatalum smelter shutdown and Aluminium Bahrain's force majeure exacerbated concerns. Shanghai futures also rose amid better Chinese inflation data.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: This video update discusses rising aluminum prices due to drying imports, tariffs, and global events impacting supply in Q1 2026. Experts cover steel mill output, the need for escalation clauses, and preparations for construction season. Aluminum market faces fast-moving price increases.

item.title: Steel & Aluminum Price Outlook – March 2026 Market Update
item.description: Steel continues to show rising mill output and strong demand, with prices expected to increase for the next 2-3 months. The update covers supply chain challenges, tariff impacts, and what contractors should expect as construction season ramps up.

item.title: Blog: The 11% question: women, talent, and sustainable steel
item.description: A blog post by Soo Jung Kim, Head of People Sustainability, explores the role of talent and workforce diversity in advancing sustainable steel production. The article reflects on over 25 years of industry experience and emphasizes that the steel sector continues to evolve.

item.title: Scrap Metal Market Recap | Aluminum 4¢ Copper 2¢
item.description: Aluminum led the scrap metal market with EC wire rising 4 cents per pound, while copper moved higher by 2 cents on the ScrapMonster Price Index. Brass, stainless, and steel prices remained unchanged during the period.

item.title: Copper theft ticks up as prices surge
item.description: Copper theft is increasing in South Florida amid surging copper prices. The report describes an 'evolution' in theft patterns linked to market trends. This highlights secondary market impacts of rising prices.

item.title: Daily Scrap Metal Market Briefing | Aluminum 4¢ Copper 2¢
item.description: Copper scrap prices saw modest gains of 2 cents per pound across key grades like number one copper wiring and light copper on the ScrapMonster Price Index. Benchmark copper traded around $5.77 per pound globally, with traders watching $5.70-$5.80 levels for near-term direction amid stable intake flows.

item.title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
item.description: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs due to fears of prolonged shipping disruptions in the Middle East from U.S.-Israel actions against Iran, impacting Gulf aluminum supplies. ADNOC's Ruwais refinery shutdown adds to energy infrastructure concerns affecting production. Broader trade tensions, including U.S. probes into excess capacity, loom over the market.

item.title: Q1 2026 Metal Pricing Trends: What Contractors Must Know
item.description: Steel prices are rising due to increased mill output and strong demand, expected to continue for the next 2-3 months. Discussion covers aluminum import issues, tariffs, global events impacting supply, and advice for contractors on escalation clauses amid Q1 trends.

item.title: Are Copper Prices About to Surge?
item.description: The International Copper Study Group forecasts refined copper use to grow 2.1% to 28.73 million metric tons in 2026, outpacing production growth and signaling potential supply tightness. This demand surge is driven by energy transition needs.

item.title: Week in review and thoughts for the upcoming week of March 9, 2026
item.description: Middle East conflicts have halted aluminum shipments from GCC producers, the second-largest supplier outside China, with Aluminium Bahrain and Qatalum declaring force majeure. European supplies are strained by additional outages in Mozambique, Iceland, and Russia. Aluminum premiums are soaring, with US Midwest at $1.12/pound and European duty-paid at $450/ton, amid bullish forecasts to $3,700/ton.

item.title: Aluminum prices surge to a four-year high amid war
item.description: Aluminum prices have surged to a four-year high amid ongoing war-related disruptions. The video discusses the rapid market movements and their implications.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News
item.description: Market update covering global steel industry news, pricing data, and analyses. Recent developments include Koç Group's partnership with Swedish green steel producer StegraİR and Nucor raising HRC prices by $5/st.

item.title: Copper Prices Jump 2% Today, After Hitting a Record High in Jan 2026
item.description: Copper prices jumped 2% on March 10, 2026, following a record high in January 2026 amid a projected 15-year global supply shortage. Existing supply constraints are keeping prices elevated, pressuring margins for cables and wires companies.

item.title: Copper price edges lower as traders await return of China demand
item.description: Copper prices dipped 0.1% to $13,304.50/t on the LME as traders await post-Lunar New Year demand rebound in China, with inventories surging to 531,700 tons at major centers, the highest since early 2020. Global exchange stockpiles are also rising amid tepid physical demand despite record highs driven by US trade policies and mine issues. Long-term bullishness persists due to supply constraints and rising demand from EVs, data centers, and renewables.

item.title: Why copper's pause may be more about policy than demand
item.description: Copper prices have cooled after rallying above $13,000 per tonne, with trade policy, US tariff risks, and shifting Chinese demand emerging as key factors shaping market direction. The consolidation phase reflects complex forces beyond electrification demand, including AI-driven energy infrastructure pressures.

item.title: LME nickel on Mar 10 gains slightly as geopolitical concerns ease, China economic indicators improve
item.description: The London Metal Exchange (LME) nickel futures price on March 10 slightly increased by US$19/ton to US$17,488/ton, with spot prices also edging up by US$14/ton. The modest gains were driven by easing geopolitical concerns and improving Chinese economic indicators.

item.title: METALMARKETUPDATE - MARCH 2026
item.description: U.S. tariffs on metals remain in flux post-Supreme Court ruling, increasing material costs by 5-15% and unit prices by 3-10%. Steel plate mill lead times extended for stainless and duplex to 9-10 weeks domestically. Nickel futures fell amid Middle East tensions, impacting industrial metals.

item.title: Aluminium prices expected to rise with prolonged war set to hit Gulf producers
item.description: Aluminum prices are likely to rise due to prolonged disruptions from war affecting Gulf producers, with forecasts up to $3,700-$4,000 per tonne amid low stocks and limited idled capacity. Bahrain's Alba declared force majeure on deliveries due to Strait of Hormuz transit issues, while Qatar’s Qatalum shut down production on March 3 from natural gas shortages. Producers may sustain operations for 3-4 weeks using on-site raw materials if the strait reopens soon.

item.title: Tariffs threaten Canadian aluminum critical to U.S. energy transition
item.description: Trump's announced doubling of tariffs on Canadian aluminum, set for March 12, threatens U.S. supplies vital for solar, wind, and EV energy transition, where demand is surging. Canadian smelters use low-carbon hydroelectric power unlike U.S. fossil fuel-dependent ones, and funding cuts jeopardize a new 'Green Smelter' project aimed at doubling domestic production with 75% lower emissions.

item.title: Steel rose to 3,107 CNY/T on March 11, 2026
item.description: Steel prices reached 3,107 CNY/T, up 0.29% from the previous day, hovering near five-week highs. China renewed its pledge to support the steel sector by curbing excess capacity, with economic planners signaling orderly reductions in steel capacity that could lift prices and improve profit margins.

item.title: Mercuria to withdraw nearly 100,000t of aluminum from LME
item.description: Commodity trader Mercuria plans to withdraw nearly 100,000 tons of aluminum from LME warehouses in Port Klang, Malaysia, amid the Strait of Hormuz shutdown freezing Middle East shipments. Middle East producers like Alba and Qatalum face production reductions and force majeure, straining supplies in Europe and the US where physical premiums have soared.

item.title: Novelis Wins Award for 100% Recycled End-Of-Life Vehicle Scrap Product
item.description: Novelis Inc. received the Münchner Management Kolloquium (MMK) Award of Excellence 2026 for developing an aluminium sheet made from 100% end-of-life vehicle scrap for exterior car body applications. The award recognizes the company's innovation in sustainable automotive material development and demonstrates the technical feasibility of car-to-car recycling for high-quality aluminium applications, representing a significant step toward decarbonizing the automotive sector.

item.title: Tariffs, demand boost US steel outlook: Nucor
item.description: Nucor reports historic order backlogs entering 2026, up nearly 40% annually in steel mills and 15% in products, supported by tariffs and demand. The company highlights ongoing issues like transshipping via Mexico and Canada under USMCA renegotiations and limited improvement in automotive and construction sectors. Overall demand is expected to rise slightly in 2026.

item.title: Major steel and raw material futures prices in China - March 10, 2026
item.description: SteelOrbis provides closing prices for major steel futures in China on March 10, 2026, including rebar at RMB 3,104/mt ($450/mt). Recent updates note rising longs prices in Turkey and Saudi Arabia amid demand, with weekly changes showing gains in rebar and HRC. The Orbis Steel Index indicates positive trends for Turkish rebar.

item.title: Aluminium prices rise as focus shifts to the Middle East
item.description: Aluminium prices recovered after a sell-off, with the Shanghai Futures Exchange contract up 2.31% to 25,275 Yuan ($3,680.81) per metric ton and LME three-month aluminum rising 0.5% to $3,423 a ton. The rise is attributed to supply disruptions in the Middle East due to conflict, affecting around 9% of global supply via the Strait of Hormuz. Trump's comments on the Iran war temporarily cooled concerns but focus shifted back.

item.title: Iran Conflict Pushes LME Aluminum Prices Higher
item.description: Middle East aluminum production disruptions from the Iran conflict have driven LME prices to a four-year high of $3,418 per tonne, with smelters like Aluminium Bahrain and Norsk Hydro's Qatalum halting operations due to suspended gas supplies. Citigroup analysts warn prices could reach $4,000 if disruptions continue. The region accounts for nearly 10% of global refined aluminum output outside China, exacerbating tight inventories.

item.title: Aluminium prices rise as focus shifts to the Middle East
item.description: Aluminum prices recovered after a sell-off, rising 2.31% to 25,275 Yuan per metric ton on the Shanghai Futures Exchange amid Middle East supply losses from conflict. Qatalum in Qatar halted production, and Aluminium Bahrain declared force majeure on shipments. Analysts note restarts could take at least half a year, signaling prolonged supply tightness.

item.title: Average global steel price in 2026 seen up on year at $625/tn, says BMI
item.description: Global steel prices for longs and flats are forecasted to average $625 per tonne in 2026, up from $600 in 2025, due to lower production especially in China. China's steel output is expected to drop 4% to 922 million tonnes amid capacity curbs. Global production may dip 0.7% year-on-year, with emerging markets like India showing growth despite tariff risks.

item.title: Aluminium rallys focus on Middle East supply disruptions
item.description: Benchmark aluminium prices on the London Metal Exchange rose 1.1% to $3,442 per metric ton amid Middle East supply disruptions. China's primary aluminum output increased 3% in January-February 2026 to 7.53 million metric tonnes, driven by higher profits from falling input costs and rising prices. Profit margins for aluminum smelters averaged 7,879 Yuan per ton.

item.title: Aluminium extends gains as supply concerns amid Mideast conflict linger
item.description: Aluminum prices surged on Thursday due to persistent global supply tightening from the ongoing Middle East conflict. Disruptions continue to fuel concerns over production capacity and availability.

item.title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
item.description: Alumichem has received a green grant to develop a circular innovation project that replaces virgin aluminium with recycled aluminium in aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) production for drinking water and wastewater purification. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (MUDP) and conducted in collaboration with Stena Recycling and Stena Aluminium, aims to significantly reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while maintaining high quality standards for drinking water applications.

item.title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
item.description: New 25% tariff rates on steel and aluminum imports into the United States are set to take effect on March 12, according to executive orders signed by President Donald Trump on Monday. A US official had previously indicated the rates would start on March 4, but the full texts of the orders were released hours after signing. These tariffs are expected to impact steel market dynamics significantly.

item.title: New steel and aluminum tariff rates take effect March 12, executive orders show
item.description: The new 25% tariffs on US steel and aluminum imports will take effect on March 12, as per executive orders signed by President Donald Trump. An earlier announcement suggested March 4, but the orders' full texts were delayed in release. This development is likely to influence steel pricing and trade flows.

item.title: Nickel Hits 19-month High
item.description: Nickel increased to 18,908.00 USD/T, the highest since June 2024. Over the past 4 weeks, nickel gained 19.58%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 21.08%.

item.title: Indonesia to cut nickel mining quota in 2026
item.description: Indonesia's energy ministry will reduce the 2026 nickel production quota to 260-270 million tonnes, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025 and below estimated consumption of 330 million tonnes. This cut aligns with market expectations but may lead to smelter production reductions or increased imports. The decision contributes to ongoing supply tightening in the nickel market.

item.title: Steel Imports Up 4.6% In January 2026 vs. December 2025
item.description: The American Iron and Steel Institute reported that steel imports to the US increased by 4.6% in January 2026 compared to December 2025. This update reflects ongoing monitoring of steel market dynamics and import trends. The data is part of AISI's regular press releases on steel shipments and imports.

item.title: Nickel Mining Industry Report 2026: Production by Country, Prices, Companies, Major Operating Mines, Competitive Landscape, Major Exploration and Development Projects, 2025-2035
item.description: Global nickel production is forecast to increase by 9.4% to 4.4 million tonnes in 2026, primarily driven by Indonesia, with smaller contributions from Canada, the Philippines, New Caledonia, and Brazil. In 2025, production rose 6.1% to 4.0 million tonnes, led by Indonesian operations like Weda Bay and others. The report covers reserves, production by country and company, major mines, and demand drivers for the nickel industry.

item.title: Nickel Mining Industry Report 2026: Production by Country, Prices, Companies, Major Operating Mines, Competitive Landscape, Major Exploration and Development Projects, 2025-2035
item.description: The 'Nickel Mining to 2035' report, released on March 12, 2026, provides comprehensive analysis of nickel production by country, pricing, key companies, operating mines, and major projects through 2035. It covers the competitive landscape and exploration developments critical for nickel's role in technology and sustainability. This latest industry report offers insights into future supply trends amid growing demand.

item.title: Aluminum Mar '26 (ALH26) - Futures - Barchart.com
item.description: Aluminum futures for March 2026 hit a period high of $3,464 on March 12, up 14.18% over the past month and 27.40% over 52 weeks. Commitment of Traders data shows shifts in commercial and non-commercial positions as of March 3.

item.title: Century Aluminum amps up American aluminum production in South Carolina
item.description: Century Aluminum is boosting U.S. aluminum production in South Carolina, supported by Trump-era tariffs. Fox Business correspondent Lydia Hu reports on the expansion efforts amid efforts to increase domestic supply. The segment aired on 'Mornings with Maria.'

item.title: March 2026 Weekly Copper Report: Prices Surpass $11000 Amid AI Infrastructure Surge And Supply Constraints
item.description: Copper prices rallied to $11,280 per metric ton amid AI data center expansion and supply disruptions from Chilean and Peruvian mines. LME stocks hit 14-month lows, supporting prices above $11,000 despite global borrowing costs. The report highlights decoupling from traditional cycles due to tech demand.

item.title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
item.description: US copper prices remain volatile in 2026 due to uncertain Chinese demand, supply shortages from lack of new mines, and slipping South American output. Record US inventories may require additional foreign production to meet demand. Forecasts vary, with Citigroup predicting over $5.90/lb by Q2 2026.

item.title: SEEC Innovation and Technology Task Force Endorses Bills to Bolster American Innovation in Clean Technologies
item.description: The House SEEC Innovation and Technology Task Force endorsed four bills to accelerate deployment of next-generation clean technologies, emphasizing US leadership in innovation for energy independence. The initiative addresses scaling breakthroughs in clean tech to counter foreign competition and combat climate challenges. It builds on principles to boost federal investments in manufacturing and research.

item.title: Nickel prices hit 15-month high on robust sentiment
item.description: Indonesia may cut its 2026 nickel ore RKAB to 250 million wet metric tonnes from 379 million in 2025, despite expected consumption rising 11% to 330 million wmt. This potential reduction contributes to nickel prices hitting a 15-month high amid robust market sentiment. The move highlights supply constraints from the world's top producer.

item.title: Aluminum prices on March 12, 2026, approached a four-year high, reaching $3,502.50 USD/ton
item.description: On March 12, 2026, LME three-month aluminum prices rose 1.32% to $3,502.50 per ton, nearing a four-year high. The increase reflects ongoing market pressures in the aluminum sector.

item.title: Aluminum prices on March 12, 2026, approached a four-year high
item.description: The LME benchmark three-month aluminum price increased 1.32% to $3,502.50 per ton on March 12, nearing a four-year high. This surge is driven by persistent supply concerns.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Surge Amid Gulf Energy Crisis
item.description: Gulf conflict is driving aluminium prices to four-year highs amid tensions affecting aluminum production. The crisis highlights vulnerabilities in global supply chains.

item.title: [SMM Nickel Midday Review] Nickel Prices Rose Slightly on March 13
item.description: On March 13, the SMM price of #1 refined nickel rose by 400 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Spot premiums for Jinchuan #1 refined nickel adjusted accordingly. This slight increase reflects ongoing market dynamics in the nickel spot market.

item.title: Major steel and raw material futures prices in China - March 13, 2026
item.description: Rebar futures at Shanghai Futures Exchange closed at RMB 3,142/mt ($455/mt), up 0.58%. Weekly changes include iron ore up 5.18%, rebar FOB Turkey up 2.26%, and HRC FOB China up 2.11%. Turkish merchant bar export prices trended up amid higher energy costs.

item.title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Copper fell to 5.63 USD/Lbs on March 13, 2026, down 3.36% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have fallen 2.32%, though still up 15.87% year-over-year. Historical high was 6.58 in January 2026.

item.title: BC Copper 2604 Closed Lower with a Wide Trading Range
item.description: BC copper prices slid straight down during the daytime session, dipping to 88,550 yuan/mt near the close before settling lower. The wide trading range was pressured by geopolitics and interest rate cut expectations.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Falls to US$17,530/Ton Level (Friday, March 13, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month forward nickel commodity price dropped to US$17,530 per ton on Friday, March 13, 2026, down from US$17,725 the previous day. Over the past week, prices fell 0.26% with an average of US$17,480 per ton, though year-to-date in 2026, prices have risen 3.64%. The article notes a five-year annual downward trend but monthly rises, with Indonesia's nickel exports projected to reach US$60 billion in 2025 due to EV downstreaming.

item.title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Nickel prices fell to 17,320 USD/T on March 13, 2026, down 2.50% from the previous day, amid a broader pullback in industrial metals. Factors include rising Middle East tensions, surging Brent crude, and a stronger dollar prompting investors to reduce exposure to cyclical metals. Monthly gains stand at 1.05%, with year-over-year increases of 4.65%.

item.title: Aluminum Prices Surge Following New 50% Tariff: What You Need to Know
item.description: Aluminum prices are rising again after U.S. tariffs on imported extrusions increased to 50% in June 2025, impacting Midwest Transaction Price, Premium, and LME benchmarks. The escalation has caused supply chain disruptions and higher costs. Market charts updated as of March 13, 2026, show marked increases across key indicators.

item.title: Alcoa Corp. (AA): The Industrial Pivot to AI Infrastructure and the Aluminum Surge
item.description: Alcoa is pivoting to AI infrastructure amid surging aluminum prices over $3,400/ton due to supply disruptions and energy demands. Innovations like ELYSIS zero-carbon smelting and Sustana low-carbon/recycled products position Alcoa for green premiums under EU CBAM. The company leverages idled sites for data centres, blending metal production with real estate value.

item.title: European Steel Industry News - Weekly Podcast - 13 March 2026
item.description: European steel markets remain stable despite Middle East tensions, with focus on carbon policy including CBAM costs and ETS reform. Volvo achieves a milestone with SSAB's green steel in serial production, boosting demand. New US trade probes on steel overcapacity and potential bidder for Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe add cross-border pressures.

item.title: Recent deep-sea and short-sea cfr Turkey scrap deals
item.description: Related news post mentions recent scrap deals impacting steel production inputs in Turkey. This ties into global steel supply chains amid ongoing market dynamics. Specific details on deals support regional capacity considerations.

item.title: Copper Declines as Strong Dollar Weighs
item.description: Copper traded just below $5.9 per pound after rising for three sessions, supported by Chinese dip-buying but weighed by a strong dollar. Annual smelting charges plunged to $0 per ton, signaling copper concentrate shortages. Exchange inventories in Shanghai capped gains despite long-term deficit narratives.

item.title: Copper Price Forecast for Next Week (9th - 13th March 2026)
item.description: Copper futures ended the week lower at 1198.20 due to a strong US dollar amid Middle East tensions and inflation fears. Global inventories surged over 500,000 tons, with Shanghai hitting records, reflecting weak Chinese demand. MCX prices declined nearly 4%, showing bearish near-term pressure.

item.title: Greenland grants 30-year permit to EU-backed Molybdenum mine
item.description: Greenland Resources received a 30-year exploitation permit for its molybdenum and magnesium project in east Greenland. The company has signed long-term supply agreements with Outokumpu and Cogne Acciai Speciali earlier this year. This development highlights ongoing interest in Greenland's mineral resources despite extraction challenges.

item.title: Conglin Aluminum Technology Expands Global Aluminum Solutions with Advanced Manufacturing and Precision Engineering
item.description: Conglin Aluminum Technology is expanding its aluminum extrusion, precision machining, and customized product development to meet global demand for lightweight, durable, and sustainable materials in transportation, construction, and other sectors. The company emphasizes sustainability through recyclable aluminum, reduced material waste, and lightweight solutions that cut vehicle weight by up to 400 kg, lowering energy consumption and emissions. It has earned recognition including the China Patent Gold Award and leads in national standards formulation.

item.title: COPPER Next Up to SHOCK the Market - 'Serious' Deficit
item.description: Ian Harris, CEO of Copper Giant, discusses copper as the next metal to rise dramatically due to supply-side challenges and accelerating AI-driven demand. The video covers trends in copper, effects of geopolitical conflicts on supply, and the company's 2026 exploration plans for their flagship Mocoa project in Colombia.

item.title: 3-Month Forward Nickel Commodity Price Drops to US$17,530/Ton Level (Friday, March 13, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month forward nickel commodity price declined to US$17,530 per ton on March 13, 2026, down from US$17,725 the previous day. Year-to-date, nickel prices have risen 3.64%, though they remain 1.04% below the year's highest price of US$17,350 per ton. Over the past week, prices decreased by 0.26% with an average daily trading price of US$17,480 per ton.

item.title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
item.description: The Nickel Institute published a series of articles on sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production. Featured content includes an interview with Dr. Mark McArthur from NOVONIX about their patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology expected to reduce costs and environmental impact of nickel-based cathode production, discussions on nickel-containing stainless steel's role in climate neutrality, and analysis of nickel's sustainability throughout the entire value chain from mining to end-of-life.

item.title: Global Aluminium Industry Outlook 2026
item.description: The video discusses 2026 aluminium demand outlook, bauxite, alumina, and primary aluminium trends, plus impacts from Middle East tensions on energy and supply. Pricing scenarios project averages of $3,400-$3,600 short-term and $3,150-$3,250 annually, with potential metal shortages.

item.title: US aluminium prices break away as tariffs collide with global supply strain
item.description: US aluminium prices exceeded $5,000 per tonne due to tariffs, falling imports, shrinking stocks, and Europe's supply disruptions. Global supply strains are exacerbating the tight market conditions.

item.title: Global Aluminium Industry Outlook 2026
item.description: This webinar discusses the global aluminium demand outlook for 2026, including bauxite, alumina, and primary aluminium market trends. It covers new smelting capacity additions of 10-12 million tons outside China, impacts from Middle East tensions, and production updates like Balco's 435,000-ton expansion. Topics also include pricing, market balance, downstream demand, and recycling challenges.

item.title: Copper Price Falls for Three Consecutive Days
item.description: Copper prices fell 1.89% to US$5.76 per pound, marking three consecutive days of declines. This drop is higher than the average weekly movement, with year-to-date growth at 0.02%. The price is down 7.2% from this year's high of US$6.2 per pound.

item.title: Copper Pressured by Dollar Strength
item.description: Copper futures fell below $5.8 per pound on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of losses. The decline was driven by a strengthening dollar.

item.title: Aluminum fell to 3,420 USD/T on March 13, 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices dropped to 3,420 USD/T on March 13, 2026, down 2.82% from the previous day, despite a 12.30% monthly rise and 27.52% yearly increase. The price reflects tightening market conditions, with Rio Tinto offering premiums to Japanese buyers. Forecasts predict trading at 3437.81 USD/T by quarter-end and 3616.04 in 12 months.

item.title: Aluminum Breaks $3,000 for First Time in Three Years as Supply Crisis Looms
item.description: South32 will place its Mozal smelter in Mozambique under care and maintenance today due to failure to secure affordable electricity, removing 240,000 tons of annual aluminum production capacity. Analysts forecast a global aluminum deficit of 200,000 tons in 2026, potentially widening to 600,000 tons if the closure is permanent. Supply constraints from China's capacity cap, Western energy issues, and strong demand from electrification support higher prices.

item.title: Copper Cables: Antitrust Launches Investigation into Alleged Cartel ... Prysmian and Nexans Stock Market Drops
item.description: Italy's Antitrust Authority has launched an investigation into major low-voltage copper cable manufacturers, including Prysmian Cavi e Sistemi Italia Srl and others, for alleged price-fixing since 2005. Inspections were conducted at company offices, distributors, and the National Federation of Electrical Material Wholesalers. Prysmian shares dropped 1.26% to 62.64 euros, while Nexans fell 1.9%, though analysts view the impact as negligible given the small market share.

item.title: Bahrain begins output cuts at world's biggest Aluminum Smelter
item.description: Aluminium Bahrain (Alba) has initiated a phased shutdown of three production lines, representing 19% of its 1.6 million tonnes annual capacity, to preserve raw materials amid Strait of Hormuz disruptions. This exacerbates industry issues from West Asia conflicts, causing price surges to four-year highs. Other smelters like Qatar's face gas shortages and shipment halts.

item.title: Slower production growth will push copper market to deficit in 2026
item.description: The International Copper Study Group (ICSG) revised its 2026 copper market forecast to a deficit of 150,000 metric tons due to slower refined production growth of 0.9% (down from expected 3.4% in 2025). Recent mine disruptions in Indonesia, Chile, and Congo have prompted downward revisions, though the ICSG expects mine output to rise 2.3% in 2026 with recovery from expanded capacity and improved output from key producing regions.

item.title: Copper price tops $12,000 for first time on tariffs, supply woes
item.description: Copper prices exceeded $12,000 per tonne for the first time on record, driven by mine disruptions across the Americas, Africa, and Asia combined with tariff concerns and rising demand from electrification and AI infrastructure. BloombergNEF forecasts the copper market could slip into deficit as early as 2026, with potential deficits reaching 19 million tonnes by 2050 without major investment in new mines and recycling.

item.title: Aluminum - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Aluminum prices fell to 3,391.20 USD/T on March 16, 2026, down 0.84% daily but up 11.54% monthly amid supply disruptions. Alba's output cuts, Strait of Hormuz closure impacting 9% of global production, and China's stalled output after exceeding its 45 million tonne cap in 2025 heighten shortage concerns. Qatar's production halt due to gas shortages adds to global supply pressures.

item.title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
item.description: Steel prices rose to 3,144 CNY/T on March 16, 2026, up 0.32% from the previous day, with a 3.66% increase over the past month despite being 2.27% lower year-over-year. Steel rebar futures held steady above CNY 3,130 per ton near six-week highs, supported by a 3.6% drop in Chinese steel output to 160 million tons in the first two months due to curtailed production amid weak demand and seasonal factors.

item.title: Steel Gains as China Curbs Overcapacity
item.description: Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,140 per ton, reaching six-week highs as China pledged to curb excess steel capacity. Economic planners at the National People’s Congress announced orderly reductions in steel capacity, potentially lifting prices and improving profit margins.

item.title: AISI: Domestic mill production eases for third week
item.description: US raw steel production declined for the third consecutive week to 1,774,000 short tons in the week ending March 13, down 17,000 tons from the prior week, though year-to-date production is up 4.9% from 2025. The mill capability utilization rate fell to 76.7%. Production decreased in four of five regions week-over-week.

item.title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
item.description: The IBAAS 2026 conference will unite global aluminium experts in India with 10 specialized technical sessions addressing bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, and AI applications. Key themes include bauxite beneficiation, red mud management, aluminium recycling, cathode technology innovations, and decarbonization strategies through renewable energy integration and Industry 4.0 digitalization.

item.title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian Team Up for Green Data Center Aluminum
item.description: Rio Tinto and Prysmian launched an industrial trial producing low-carbon aluminium cables for data centers using a blend of hydropowered aluminium and ELYSIS® technology, which eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from smelting. The five-year supply agreement combines both companies' technologies to help customers reduce the carbon footprint of IT infrastructure.

item.title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian partner on low-carbon aluminum solutions for data centers
item.description: Rio Tinto and Prysmian partnered on an industrial trial to produce low-carbon aluminum cables for the growing data center market using ELYSIS technology and hydropowered smelting. The collaboration aims to support the data center industry's expansion while helping customers meet sustainability objectives and reduce their carbon footprint.

item.title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
item.description: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduced new industry innovation awards recognizing standout innovations in Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, Automation & Connectivity. The awards recognize implemented solutions, pilot projects, and prototypes from the past 48 months, aiming to reward companies driving meaningful change in the aluminium sector.

item.title: Copper price eases as traders weigh Fed's next move
item.description: Copper is consolidating after a two-month surge driven by speculative buying that took it to a record high at the end of January. Minutes from the Fed's January meeting indicated policymakers were wary of cutting rates, which weighed on the demand outlook for metals. Renewed US-Iran risks also pressured demand for risky assets including metals.

item.title: [SMM News Flash] On March 17, SMM reported that the ex-factory...
item.description: Nickel prices and stainless steel futures remained elevated this week, with bullish sentiment driving continuous increases in high-grade NPI (nickel pig iron) prices. The market continues to show strength amid ongoing supply and demand dynamics.

item.title: Aluminum prices in March 2026
item.description: Aluminum prices on LME have surged over 22% year-to-date in 2026, outpacing gold's 15% rise, amid Middle East geopolitical tensions. The Strait of Hormuz, vital for 20% of global aluminum production outside China, is a key factor driving the shock increase.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Signals Industrial Resurgence: Q1 2026 Guidance Nearly Doubles Previous Year's Performance
item.description: Steel Dynamics issued strong Q1 2026 guidance, signaling robust demand outstripping domestic production capacity amid infrastructure and data center booms. Mini-mill operators like Steel Dynamics and Nucor are poised to benefit from onshoring trends and federal funding. Imported steel-reliant manufacturers may face higher costs.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Provides First Quarter 2026 Earnings Guidance
item.description: Steel Dynamics forecasts Q1 2026 earnings of $2.73 to $2.77 per diluted share, driven by higher steel shipments, metal margin expansion, and strong demand in non-residential construction, energy, automotive, and industrial sectors. Steel fabrication earnings remain steady with a 35% higher order backlog, supported by infrastructure and onshoring. The company advances commissioning of its new aluminum flat rolled products mill in Mississippi.

item.title: Steel Dynamics Provides First Quarter 2026 Earnings Guidance
item.description: Steel Dynamics expects higher Q1 2026 profitability from steel operations due to increased shipments and expanded margins, with strong demand from key sectors. Metals recycling earnings rise on higher selling values despite lower scrap shipments from weather impacts. Fabrication volumes grow with a significantly larger order backlog into Q3 2026 amid infrastructure support.

item.title: Copper futures fell as Chinese demand softened
item.description: Copper futures declined on March 17, 2026, amid weakening physical demand in China, with fabricator buying slumping and the Yangshan import premium at its lowest since mid-2024. This marked the fourth decline in five trading days, reversing a brief rally as traders focused on softened consumption.

item.title: [SMM Daily Review of Nickel Sulphate] March 17, Trading Sentiment Was Relatively Weak, and Nickel Salt Prices Remained Stable
item.description: Trading sentiment for nickel sulphate was relatively weak on March 17. Nickel salt prices held steady despite the subdued market mood. This stability occurred amid broader nickel market fluctuations.

item.title: Global Iron and Steel Prices and News 18.03.2026
item.description: SteelRadar provides updates on global iron and steel prices, including steady Shanghai Futures rebar and HRC prices on March 17, 2026. Key news highlights Türkiye as a transit hub for Chinese steel to Europe, Czech steel industry crisis, and Nucor raising HRC prices by $5/st. CBAM deadline of March 31 poses risks for non-compliant companies.

item.title: Leading Companies Fueling Growth and Innovation in the Copper Foil Market
item.description: The copper foil market is forecasted to reach $22.54 billion by 2030 with a 10.0% CAGR, driven by advancements in high-performance copper foils for energy storage and electronics. Industry innovations include AI-powered quality control, IoT-connected manufacturing lines, and sustainable copper foil manufacturing practices, with strategic partnerships like Varta and SK Nexilis advancing EV battery technologies.

item.title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Rises Toward US$17,420/Ton Level (Tuesday, March 17, 2026)
item.description: The 3-month forward nickel price rose 0.49% to US$17,420 per ton on March 17, 2026, marking the highest weekly jump and a 2.99% year-to-date gain. Weekly average prices stood at US$17,480 per ton with 0.2% growth. Annually, prices trend downward over five years, but monthly monitoring shows rises, peaking at US$18,030 in January 2026.

item.title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
item.description: Prysmian shares rose as much as 4% on expectations that the world's largest cable maker's large U.S. business could benefit from U.S. tariffs on copper, as it sources the metal locally and can pass on higher prices. Copper is key for power grids, construction, and high-voltage cables, with Prysmian as the largest global buyer and U.S. leader. Analysts at Citi highlight its vertically integrated U.S. setup using local copper from Arizona and Texas as a competitive edge.

item.title: Acciaierie d'Italia to start preferential negotiations with Azerbaijani consortium on acquisition of former Ilva plants
item.description: Extraordinary commissioners of Acciaierie d’Italia and ILVA in extraordinary administration have requested authorization from Italy’s Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy to begin preferential negotiations with an Azerbaijani consortium (Baku Steel Company CJSC and Azerbaijan Investment Company OJSC) for the sale of the former Ilva steel plants.[8] Ten proposals had been received by the January 10 deadline, and the chosen consortium was selected based on criteria including financial stability, industrial sustainability, and expected benefits for employment and local communities.[8]
